title: Security
questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'security'?
Do you have all the security you need?
What would life be like with little security?
Do you get nervous going through airport security?
What do you think of world security since the 9-11 attacks?
What do you do to make sure your Internet security is strong?
What does the UN Security Council do? How effective is it?
Does your country provide good social security if you have no job or home?
Douglas MacArthur said: "There is no security on this earth. Only opportunity." Do you agree with this?
Franklin Delano Roosevelt said: "True individual freedom cannot exist without economic security and independence." Do you agree?
What kinds of security are there in life?
How important is financial security to you?
What kinds of security measures are there in your home?
Do you worry about job security?
Do you always download security patches from Microsoft?
What do you think of airline security?
Do you have to have security in a relationship?
If you had to choose between security and freedom, which would you go for?
Someone once said: "Security is an illusion. Life is either a daring adventure or it is nothing at all." Do you agree?
Britain's Lord Acton said: "The most certain test by which we judge whether a country is really free is the amount of security enjoyed by minorities." Do you agree with this?
